content.problemStatement:
Write a program to calculate the total marks obtained in three subjects where the marks may include decimal values (`85.5`, `90.0` & `88.3`). Use the addition (`+`) operator and print the total marks with one decimal place.

**Hint**: Use %.1f to print a float value with 1 digit after the decimal point. (We will learn more about such formatting in the float topic.)
constraints: Each mark is given with at most one digit after the decimal point
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains three decimal numbers (marks) separated by spaces.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a line in the format: Total Marks = <total> where <total> is the sum of the three marks printed with exactly one digit after the decimal point.

solution.approach: Read the integer T. For each test case read three floating‑point numbers, add them using double precision and print the result using a format that forces exactly one digit after the decimal point (e.g., %.1f in C, fixed<<setprecision(1) in C++, String.format("%.1f") in Java, f"{value:.1f}" in Python, toFixed(1) in JavaScript). The algorithm runs in O(T) time and uses O(1) extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) total, O(1) per test case

code.Python3:
import sys

def main():
    raw = sys.stdin.read()
    raw = raw.replace('\\n', ' ')
    data = raw.strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    t = int(data[0])
    idx = 1
    out_lines = []
    for _ in range(t):
        a = float(data[idx]); b = float(data[idx+1]); c = float(data[idx+2])
        idx += 3
        out_lines.append(f"Total Marks = {a+b+c:.1f}")
    sys.stdout.write('\\n'.join(out_lines))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
